raw_transcript_text:
DOCTOR: Namaste! Aaiye, aaiye. Kya problem hai aapko?

PATIENT: Namaste doctor sahab. Sir, mere dono haathon pe aur thoda sa pairon pe bhi rash aa gaya hai. Bahut khujli hoti hai.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, pahle dikhaiye. Haan, haath dikhaye. (examines) Kab se hai yeh?

PATIENT: Sir, pichhale do hafte se ho raha hai. Pehle chhota sa tha, ab phail gaya hai.

DOCTOR: Koi aur symptom? Bukhar to nahi hai? Ya aankhon mein jalna?

PATIENT: Nahi, bukhar to nahi. Haan, aankhon mein aur gale mein bhi halki khujli si hoti hai kabhi kabhi.

DOCTOR: Theek hai. Khana khane ke baad kuchh aisa lagta hai ki zyada hota hai? Koi particular food trigger karta hai?

PATIENT: Haan, mujhe bada shaq hai egg se. Pehle bhi egg khane ke baad halka reaction hua tha, par itna nahi.

DOCTOR: Hmm. Koi aur dawai toh nahi le rahe the? Pain killer? Ya koi antibiotic? Jaise Dolo, Augmentin, Azithral—ye sab?

PATIENT: Haan sir, do hafte pehle mujhe joint pain mein Dolo-650 liya tha, lekin ek baar hi.

DOCTOR: Theek hai. Aur haan, BP? Sugar? Koi purani bimari?

PATIENT: Sugar nahi hai, BP bhi normal hai. Pichhali baar jab mera checkup hua tha tab 120/80 tha.

DOCTOR: Chalo, vitals check karte hain. (checks BP) Aapka BP abhi one twenty two over eighty hai, theek hai. Pulse bhi normal hai. Koi pet ki problem? Kabz?

PATIENT: Haan, kabz kabhi kabhi ho jaati hai. Khaana bhi normal schedule pe nahi rehta.

DOCTOR: Koi na. Ab hum skin ko dekhte hain. (examines) Yeh rash ek typical urticaria jaise hai — allergic reaction lagta hai. Gharelu mahol mein bhi koi change aaya hai? Koi nayaa soap, tootpaste, ya koi pet?

PATIENT: Nahi, sab puraana hai. Par haan, office mein naye tiles bichhe the pichhale hafte, shayad uss se bhi ho sakta hai?

DOCTOR: Ho sakta hai. Thoda allergic contact dermatitis bhi lag raha hai, urticaria ke saath. Chalo, kuchh dawai likhte hain.

Prescription:

1. **Azithral 500** – 1 tablet, once daily, only if needed for infection. Given mild erythroderma, no need as of now. Avoid.

2. **Levocetirizine** – 1 tablet, at night, for 14 days. (A. C. for allergy)

3. **Shelcal 500** – 1 tablet daily with food – calcium + vitamin D (for skin healing)

4. **Pan-D** – 1 cap, empty stomach morning, for 14 days (for digestion and to avoid gastritis from antihistamine)

5. **Topical: Mometasone furoate 0.1% cream** – apply on rash only, 2 times a day for 7 days

PATIENT: Theek hai doctor. Aur koi test?

DOCTOR: Haan, ek allergy panel test karwa lena – serum IgE and specific IgE for common Delhi allergens like dust mite, pollen, egg. Hum ek lab ka form de rahe hoon. Koi tension nahi. Seven days baad follow up mein dikha dena.

PATIENT: Theek hai. Aur kya dhyan rakhna hai?

DOCTOR: Koi baat nahi. Egg, maach, rehta junk food avoid karein. Fresh juice, halka khana. Aur haan, agar zyada kharab lage to emergency mein dikha dena. Theek hai?

PATIENT: Haan sir, shukriya.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, namaste!

soap_note.subjective: Patient reports rash on both hands and feet for 2 weeks, with severe itching. Initially small, now spreading. Occasional mild itching in eyes and throat. No fever. Suspects egg as trigger, had prior mild reaction to egg. Took Dolo-650 once for joint pain 2 weeks ago. No known history of diabetes or hypertension; last BP was 120/80. Occasional constipation. Irregular meal schedule. No change in soap or toothpaste. May be exposed to new tiles at office 1 week ago.
soap_note.objective: BP 122/80 mmHg, pulse normal. Skin examination reveals rash consistent with urticaria and possible allergic contact dermatitis. Mild erythroderma noted.
soap_note.assessment: Allergic reaction, likely urticaria with possible allergic contact dermatitis. Suspected triggers: egg, new tiles at office. Differential includes drug reaction (Dolo-650).
soap_note.plan: 1. Azithral 500: 1 tablet once daily only if needed for infection - avoid as per current assessment. 2. Levocetirizine: 1 tablet at night for 14 days. 3. Shelcal 500: 1 tablet daily with food. 4. Pan-D: 1 capsule empty stomach in morning for 14 days. 5. Mometasone furoate 0.1% cream: apply on rash twice daily for 7 days. Recommend allergy panel test: serum IgE and specific IgE for common allergens (dust mite, pollen, egg). Avoid egg, fish, and junk food. Follow up in 7 days. Advise to seek emergency if symptoms worsen.
